ops.title: The codex skill returns empty (\"Skill execution completed\", no output) inside Workflow subagent wrappers; run it from the main session instead
ops.body:
During the anchors-r2 review loop (2026-07-07), sonnet-low wrapper agents inside a Workflow invoked the codex skill and got back only "Skill execution completed" with no findings text and no session under ~/.codex/sessions — across multiple wrappers and retries. The same skill invoked directly from the main session worked perfectly (full finder + refuter output).

**Why:** the codex skill's forked execution appears not to survive the workflow-subagent context; wrappers then report CODEX-UNAVAILABLE and refuter passes silently degrade.

**How to apply:** for codex review lanes in a review loop, either run the codex skill inline from the main (fable) session per lens, or verify one wrapper produces real codex output before fanning out. Budget for main-session codex calls when writing review workflows; keep wrapper prompts as a fallback only after confirming the fork works.
